ModMetaData.name: Pawn Diary (Beta)
ModMetaData.author: aimmlegate
ModMetaData.description:
BETA / WORK IN PROGRESS - expect rough edges, changing defaults, and ongoing prompt tuning; output quality depends on your model and settings.
Pawn Diary gives RimWorld colonists a private journal. It watches meaningful colony moments - social interactions, fights, mental breaks, raids, medicine, crafting, work, mood events, thoughts, arrivals, deaths, and end-of-day reflections - then asks a local or OpenAI-compatible language model to rewrite them as short diary pages in each colonist's own voice.
Entries appear in a per-colonist Diary tab. Two-colonist events get paired points of view, so each pawn can remember the same moment differently. Arrivals and deaths bookend a pawn's story with neutral pages, and optional writing styles shape each voice without touching gameplay.
LLM SUPPORT Works with any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint, plus the OpenAI Responses API. Built around compact prompts for small to mid-size local models (roughly 4-32B) through tools such as LM Studio, llama.cpp, and Ollama. Default endpoint: http://localhost:1234. Configure endpoints, models, API keys, concurrency, prompts, writing styles, and event groups in the mod settings.
Storytelling only: it does not change gameplay, add needs/hediffs/jobs, or write external save data beyond diary entries stored in your RimWorld save. Safe to add to an existing save; removing it stops diary capture and hides the Diary UI/history without leaving custom gameplay defs/components behind. Targets base RimWorld 1.6 and keeps paid DLC content optional.
